config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society's interests matter, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse by the state or by the majority. A good society protects people even when their rights are inconvenient to others, especially for minorities and unpopular voices. From my perspective, the best balance is to limit individual freedom only when there is a clear, necessary public reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika kita sudah terlalu sering melihat alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu, terutama bagi minoritas dan suara yang tidak populer, justru berfungsi sebagai pagar agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi menurut saya hanya layak diprioritaskan ketika ada ancaman nyata dan pembatasannya benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan keteraturan, melainkan tirani mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I oppose the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia that kind of logic can quickly turn into “for the common good” being used to silence dissent or excuse abuse. We do have a strong sense of gotong royong, but that should not mean the majority gets to trample minority rights or personal freedoms whenever it is convenient. Society is healthiest when individual rights are protected first, and only limited in a clear, necessary, and proportionate way for real public reasons. If we reverse that priority, I think we invite abuse more than we create harmony.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Amerika, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyensoran, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kuasa negara yang berlebihan, dan itu justru merusak kepercayaan publik. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; hak itu batas penting agar mayoritas tidak menginjak minoritas atau suara yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat memang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi hanya jika ancamannya nyata, tujuannya jelas, dan pembatasannya benar-benar sempit serta dapat diawasi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified in the name of harmony. From an Indonesian perspective, I do value gotong royong and social responsibility, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can become a tool to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or excuse heavy-handed state action. Society is stronger when individual rights are protected first, and only limited when there is a clear, necessary, and proportionate public reason. If we put society above the individual by default, I think we weaken the very trust and fairness that society depends on.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas default di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya slogan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau kuasa negara yang berlebihan, dan itu justru merusak kebebasan yang seharusnya dilindungi. Hak individu bukan lawan dari masyarakat; bagi saya, hak itu adalah pagar yang mencegah mayoritas menggilas minoritas dan suara yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat memang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi hanya kalau ancamannya nyata, pembatasannya sempit, dan ada akuntabilitas yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
